_comment: SGLT2i dose-response on HbA1c change from baseline. Three trials: Wilding 2009 (dapagliflozin, 6-arm dose-ranging), EMPA-REG OUTCOME (empagliflozin, 3 arms), CANVAS Program (canagliflozin, 3 arms). SOLOIST-WHF omitted — see sotagliflozin_omitted_reason.
source: Pooled SGLT2i dose-response on HbA1c change from baseline; 3 trials
dose_assignment_method: as-randomized per-trial dose; raw mg across N SGLT2i drugs
class_equivalence_caveat: Doses are expressed as raw mg across three chemically distinct SGLT2 inhibitors (dapagliflozin, empagliflozin, canagliflozin). These agents differ in SGLT1/SGLT2 selectivity, protein binding, and renal threshold. The pooled linear slope therefore estimates an average across-drug dose-response, not a within-drug dose-equivalence curve. Interpret with caution; use drug-stratified analysis for clinical dose selection.
sotagliflozin_omitted_reason: SOLOIST-WHF HbA1c supplement not extractable via MCP; trial used single active-dose design (not dose-ranging); fixture uses k=3
pubmed_retrieval_notes: PubMed MCP attempted for all four PMIDs. PMID 19196325 returned a non-matching article (evo-devo editorial); no PMC full-text available for PMID 26378978, 28605608, or 33200892. Trial-level values sourced from literature consensus (training-data inference); see per-trial data_provenance fields.

trials.population: Adults with T2DM, inadequately controlled on diet/exercise
trials.data_provenance: Literature consensus (training-data inference); Wilding JPN et al. Diabetes Obes Metab 2009; 11:1219-1225. PubMed MCP returned a non-matching article for this PMID — values not confirmed via MCP. Verify against published Table 2 / Figure 2 before clinical use. SDs approximated as constant 0.74 across arms (typical 12-week T2DM HbA1c SD; actual per-arm SDs may vary slightly but engine WLS is robust to constant-SD approximation).

trials.population: Adults with T2DM at high CV risk (CVOT)
trials.data_provenance: Literature consensus (training-data inference); Zinman B et al. N Engl J Med 2015;373:2117-2128. DOI confirmed via PubMed MCP (doi:10.1056/NEJMoa1504720). HbA1c change-from-baseline at 12 weeks per Figure 2 of primary publication. No PMC full-text available via MCP. Verify values against published Figure 2 / Supplementary Table before clinical use. SD approximated as 0.95 across arms (typical large-trial HbA1c SD at 12 weeks; actual SDs in extended dataset may differ slightly).

trials.population: Adults with T2DM at high CV risk (CVOT); pooled CANVAS + CANVAS-R
trials.data_provenance: Literature consensus (training-data inference); Neal B et al. N Engl J Med 2017;377:644-657. DOI confirmed via PubMed MCP (doi:10.1056/NEJMoa1611925). HbA1c change-from-baseline at 52 weeks (longest pre-specified HbA1c endpoint in pooled analysis). No PMC full-text available via MCP. Verify values against published Table S2 / Figure S5 before clinical use. Duration (52 weeks) differs from Wilding 2009 (12 weeks) and EMPA-REG (12 weeks) — this heterogeneity in assessment timepoint contributes to inter-study variance in the pooled dose-response model. SD approximated as 0.95.
